_meta.author: refine-scorecard (evidence sweep)
_meta.note: Finalize Matt Morgan (TX HD-26, R, freshman elected 2024, sworn 2025-01-14). Clear unrefined archetype TRUEs; score only documented cells. Evidence: TRTL 96 pro-life score, Texas Alliance for Life 93%, Texas Values Faith & Family 94% (Champion), TFR fiscal index 91, recorded SB2 (school choice/ESA) YES vote + tabled Talarico referendum amendment. Campaign site = vague vibes (null). 2026: incumbent, won R primary 3/3/2026, general vs Elizabeth Markowitz (D) 11/3/2026.

set.office: Texas State Representative (HD-26, Fort Bend Co.)

profile.background: Insurance adjuster / small-business owner (policyholder-side claims). B.B.A., Sam Houston State University (2002). Born 1979, Houston. Beat incumbent Jacey Jetton in 2024 R primary, won HD-26 general with 59.2%. Sworn 2025-01-14. Committees: Insurance; Land & Resource Management.
profile.confidence_note: Web-verified 2026-06-19: incumbent HD-26; advanced from R primary 3/3/2026; general 11/3/2026 vs Elizabeth Markowitz (D). Identity disambiguated from Matt Morgan (VA Delegate). Scored from 4 issue-group scorecards + recorded SB2 vote; campaign site had no specific policy positions.

matt-morgan.notes_set: Fort Bend County (Katy/Richmond/Rosenberg/Sugar Land area). Freshman R, 89th Leg (sworn 2025-01-14); first elected 2024 over incumbent Jacey Jetton. HARD issue-group evidence: Texas Right to Life 2025 pro-life score 96; Texas Alliance for Life 2025 score 93%; Texas Values Action 2025 Faith & Family score 94% (90%+ = 'Faith & Family Champion'); Texans for Fiscal Responsibility 2025 Index 91 (pro-taxpayer). Recorded vote: YES on SB 2 (universal ESA/school choice) and YES to table the Talarico referendum amendment (The Texan vote list, 2025). Sierra Club 2025 environmental score 31% (context). Campaign site (morganfortexas.com) and Houston Conservative Forum profile stated only vague themes (lower property taxes, secure border, God-given freedoms) = no scoreable position. Many subordinate cells (paper ballots, NFA/GCA repeal, CBDC, no-fault divorce, birthright) NO EVIDENCE FOUND -> null.

0.note: Texas Right to Life 2025 pro-life score 96 and Texas Alliance for Life 93%; TRTL endorsed his 2024 campaign. Pro-life voting record documents affirmation of life from conception.

1.note: High pro-life score but NO specific evidence he voted for/advocates abortion abolition (vs restrictions); not claimable.

2.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND on embryonic stem cell / IVF embryo discard / chimeric research.

3.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND on euthanasia / assisted suicide.

4.note: Pro-life-endorsed R with 96/93% pro-life ratings; no record of PP/NARAL/EMILY's List funding. Texas Values scorecard credits ending taxpayer-funded abortion travel.

0.note: Texas Values 94% covers marriage/family bills but no isolated cell on one-man-one-woman marriage statement; not separately documented.

1.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND on specific same-sex marriage/civil union position.

2.note: Texas Values 2025 Faith & Family score 94% ('Champion'); scorecard credits the Women's Privacy Act (SB 8) and legislation defining 'man'/'woman' in Texas law -- affirming immutable biological sex.

3.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND on no-fault divorce reform.

4.note: 94% Faith & Family Champion on a scorecard scoring votes opposing LGBTQ ideology in policy/schools (sex-separated spaces, defining sex).

0.note: Recorded YES vote on SB 2 (universal Education Savings Account / school choice, 2025) and YES to table the Talarico anti-ESA referendum amendment.

1.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND specific to parental consent on minor medical/gender interventions (likely in 94% F&F aggregate but not isolable).

3.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND on his vote on porn age-verification.
